sheets.name: Executive Summary
data: Premium Fabric Import Buyer Leadbook — cleaned shortlist
Purpose: remove smaller/directory-style leads from the uploaded workbook and rank the remaining companies by scale, influence, export/manufacturing relevance, and contact usability.
Use these for actual outreach first
Call carefully; bigger deal potential, more gatekeeping
Start with the 'Top 30 Strategic Targets' sheet. These are the strongest economic/influence targets.
Use city sheets for area-by-area outreach. The top ranks in each city are better than lower ranks.
Do not send price sheets first. Start with permission, then send 3–5 fabric references and ask for fabric categories / monthly volumes.
If a contact is a landline or directory-gated number, ask for the procurement / fabric sourcing / merchandising person.
The 'Removed / Backup Rows' sheet is not for first outreach. It exists only to show what was cut and why.

sheets.name: Scoring Method
data: Kept only companies that look materially stronger for a bulk fabric export discussion: exporter, manufacturer, textile mill, large corporate/brand buyer, fabric importer/distributor, or processing ecosystem lead.
Scale/economic influence, official website/public source strength, export/manufacturing category, fabric-buying relevance, public contact usability, and likelihood of reaching sourcing/procurement.

The uploaded workbook and public web sources rarely prove exact import tonnage from China/Thailand company-by-company. The workbook therefore distinguishes confirmed public scale from inferred import-fit. No private/hidden WhatsApp numbers were invented.
Message/call first; ask if they source imported fabrics; send 3–5 images + website; ask fabric categories and monthly volumes; only then send profile/price sheet.
Highest potential: listed/large exporters, textile majors, known corporate-scale manufacturers. Bigger deal value but harder gatekeeping.
Good commercial outreach targets: established exporters/manufacturers/importers/fabric ecosystem leads. Better response probability than giants.
Backup targets: plausible buyers but not clearly top-tier. Use after A/B outreach or to test messaging.
